item.title: OOP 一种软件开发大众心理 | OOP a software development mass psychosis
item.description:
广义的外行对精神病的定义大致如下： 大规模精神病的定义是当很多人分享这种虚假的信念时。

历史充满了大规模的精神病事件，今天在任何方面都没有什么不同。 历史上我最喜欢的大规模精神病事件是 500 年前在德国发生的“跳舞瘟疫” ，当时成千上万的人在没有明显原因的情况下因筋疲力尽而跳舞至死。 大约 30 年前，俄罗斯当局不得不关闭几个地区的小学，以遏制他们的“笑瘟疫” ，这种瘟疫开始在一所小学在青少年和儿童中蔓延，导致孩子们笑得无法自拔。

面向对象编程是个好东西

事实上，30 多年的历史告诉我们恰恰相反。 例如 ...

  • 在 OOP 中实现封装会导致不必要的复杂性，并且几乎无法理解代码
  • 多态性导致混乱并且极难追踪错误
  • 将数据（字段）与逻辑（方法）耦合是灾难的根源，并且以可以说是疯狂的定义的方式纠缠你的逻辑和数据
  • 单一职责会产生 1,000 多个类，这些类本来可以用 FP 中的 5 个函数完成。

我可以继续提到数百个有关 OOP 的此类问题。 然而，证据在于设计模式、干净的架构和 SOLID。 如果 OOP 真的是任何事情的解决方案，我们就不需要设计模式、干净的架构或 SOLID 设计原则。 我曾经听过一位 LISP 开发人员证明，GoF 书中 23 种原始设计模式中有 19 种在 LISP 中完全没有意义。 实际上，您可以根据编程语言所需的设计模式数量来量化编程语言的“精神病”数量，以便正确使用该语言。 如果你这样做了，你会意识到 99% 的设计模式只是围绕 OOP 的不足之处进行的“hacks” 。

当然，对于刚开始编码的开发人员来说，已经阅读了所有面向 OOP 的营销废话，将事实与虚构区分开来变得非常困难。 因此高级开发人员有责任制止这种精神病，并向初级开发人员解释更多函数式编程语言的优势，这样我们就可以有希望集体抛弃这种最初源于 50 多年前奥斯陆 Simula 67 的范式—— OOP 那当然是...

矛盾的是，相同的步骤几乎可以应用于这个世界上的任何事情，而第一步总是如下......

第一步，意识到你有问题

问题当然是面向对象编程，实现点是你大声说出这句话的时间点，不怕后果——承认面向对象实际上是一种精神病，无论如何都不是“出色的软件开发范式”怎么回事。 为了大声喊叫，每个计算机进程都包含或多或少完全相同的部分。 输入+过程产生输出。 用动词交换前一句中的过程，您已经有效地说明了曾经创建的每一个（成功的）计算机过程。

在动词方面，函数式编程在存在的各个方面都优于带有“主题”的 OOP。 所以在我之后说如下......

这不是解决方案，而是问题！

作为记录，如果你想使用一种理智的编程语言，那里有几十种不错的语言，例如......

  • 或者我个人最喜欢的（无耻插件）当然是 Hyperlambda

谈到 OOP，请问自己以下问题； 拒绝吧！



原文:

A broad layman's definition of a psychosis is roughly as follows; "The belief in something that has been proven to be wrong". The definition of a mass psychosis is when a lot of people shares this fake belief.

History is full of mass psychotic incidents, and today is not different in any ways. My favourite mass psychotic incident from history was "the dancing plague" from 500 years back in time in Germany, when literally thousands of people danced themselves to death from exhaustion without no apparent reasons what so ever. Some 30 years ago, Russian authorities has to close down primary schools in several districts to contain their "laughing plague" that started spreading at one primary school amongst teenagers and kids, resulting in children laughing so hard they could barely breadth.

For 30+ years, software developers have been taught a mantra that over time has turned into more or less the declaration of faith required to believe in to be able to land a job as a software developer, and this mantra is as follows.

Object Oriented Programming is a good thing

When in fact 30+ years of history has taught us the exact opposite. For instance ...

  • Implementing encapsulation in OOP results in unnecessary complexity and virtually impossible to understand code
  • Polymorphism results in chaos and extremely hard to track down bugs
  • Coupling data (fields) with logic (methods) is the recipe for disaster, and entangles your logic and data in ways that are arguably the very definition of madness
  • Single responsibility results in 1,000+ classes for something that could have been done with 5 functions in FP.

I could go on and mention hundreds of such issues with OOP. However the proof is in design patterns, clean architecture, and SOLID. If OOP was a solution to anything really, we wouldn't need design patterns, clean architecture, or SOLID design principles. OOP itself would be enough for us. The fact that OOP need crutches to stand upright, is by itself enough proof for us.

I once heard a LISP developer prove how 19 of the 23 original design patterns from GoF's book made absolutely no sense what so ever in LISP. In fact, you can quantify a programming language's amount of "psychosis" according to its required number of design patterns in order to correctly use the language. If you do, you will realise that 99% of all design patterns are simply "hacks" around OOP's inadequacies.

Of course for a developer who just started out coding, having read all the marketing gibberish for OOP, separating the truth from fiction becomes incredibly hard. It's therefor the responsibility of the senior developer to stop this psychosis, and explain the advantages of more functional programming languages to junior developers, such that we can hopefully collectively discard this paradigm that originally came out of Simula 67 in Oslo more than 50 years ago - OOP that is of course ...

AA have a 12 step program for healing yourself from addiction. Paradoxically, the same steps can be applied to almost anything in this world, and the first step is always as follows ...

Step 1, realise you have a problem

The problem of course is OOP, and the realisation point is the point in time when you utter this out loud, not afraid of the consequences - Admitting that OO is in fact a psychosis, and not a "brilliant software development paradigm" in any ways what so ever. For crying out loud, every single computer process consists of more or less the exact same parts; Input + process results in output. Exchange process in the former sentence with a verb, and you've effectively illustrated every single (successful) computer process that was ever created.

When it comes to verbs, functional programming is simply superior to OOP with its "subjects", in every regard that exists. So say after me as follows ...

OOP is a psychosis! It is not a solution, it is the problem!

For the record, if you want to work in a sane programming language, there exists dozens of nice languages out there, such as for instance ...

  • LISP
  • GoLang (yup, no OO here)
  • F#
  • Or my personal favourite (shameless plug) of course Hyperlambda

When it comes to OOP, ask yourself the following; "What would Ronald Reagan and Nancy Reagan do?" - The answer of course is simple ...

Just say no!
item.author: polterguy

item.title: 音乐星期一——你在听什么？ (May 2) （5月2日） | Music Monday — What are you listening to? (May 2)
item.description:
封面图片来源： Thoka Maer

在这个每周系列中，人们可以加入并删除他们正在收听的任何内容的链接 + 浏览其他建议。

如果您喜欢谈论音乐，请考虑关注 org #music 讨论以获取更多关于音乐的对话！

那么，你们今天都在听什么？ 注意：您可以使用以下语法在大多数平台上嵌入指向您歌曲的链接{% embed https://... %} 。

期待听听大家的建议！ 👂 🎶 👂🎶



原文:

cover image credits: Thoka Maer

In this weekly series, folks can chime in and drop links to whatever it is they're listening to + browse others suggestions.

If you like talking about music, consider following the org #music discussions for more conversations about music!

So, what have y'all been listening to today? Note: you can embed a link to your song on most platforms using the following syntax {% embed https://... %}.

I look forward to listening to y'all's suggestions! 👂 🎶
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 如何成为开源开发者？ | How to Become an Open Source Developer ?
item.description:
这个问题是针对使用过大量/重要的开源代码的经验丰富的开发人员提出的。

想要深入学习编程以至于他们可以为自己的开源项目贡献代码/维护自己的开源项目的人可以采取哪些可行的步骤。 即如何从菜鸟那里得到 -> [Evan You, TJ Holowaychuk , Dan Abramov, Hakim El Hattab, Anthony Fu, Chris Harris 等]

阿布舍克·塔普利亚尔—— Mar 28 3月28日



原文:

#DISCUSSION

This question goes out to experienced devs who've worked with sizeable/nontrivial open source code.

What actionable steps can someone who wants to learn programming intimately to the point that they could contribute code to/ maintain their own open source project. (stuff like clean code, design patterns, project management, domain knowledge)

ie how to get from noob -> [Evan Yoú, TJ Holowaychuk, Dan Abramov, Hakim El Hattab, Anthony Fu, Chris Harris etc]

🥲 please no vague/"it depends" kinda answers

Abhishek Thapliyal - Mar 28
item.author: free_one

item.title: 处理 Remix 中的错误 | Handling errors in Remix
item.description:
如果您的应用程序抛出无法预料的错误，这总是很烦人。

一般来说，尽早构建最常见的错误是一个很好的建议。 但是，可能会出现一些您不能总是看到的通用错误。

对我们来说幸运的是，Remix 将捕获大部分这些错误，并将它们呈现到我们定义的最接近的ErrorBoundary框。

创建根错误边界

从我的角度来看，你总是想在你的代码中添加一个根错误边界，这是最顶层，所以如果一个错误被抛出顶层，这个错误总是能够捕捉到它。

要创建这些根错误边界之一，请打开root.tsx文件。

根据文档，最好为根错误边界呈现完整的 HTML，因为它将在此错误的呈现上安装和卸载。
通过访问<Meta />, <Links />, and <Script />标签，它可能能够重新渲染。

让我们在这个根文件中添加以下函数：

 export function ErrorBoundary ({ error }) { return ( < html > < head > < title > Oh no !< /title > < Meta /> < Links /> < /head > < body className = ' m-4 ' > < h1 className = ' text-2xl ' > Something went wrong !< /h1 > < p > { error . message } < /p > < Scripts /> < /body > < /html > ); }

让我们尝试一下，看看会发生什么； 我在app/routes/admin/posts/index.tsx添加了这个特殊错误。

 export function loader () { throw new Error ( ' I am a failure! ' ); }

如果我们现在打开这个页面，我们会遇到以下错误。

这已经比不抛出自定义错误要好得多。
缺点是阻塞了整个页面，而我们的错误只是被抛出。

添加嵌套错误边界

这就是 Remix 让我大吃一惊的地方。 它支持多个错误边界！

我们可以在我们在我们的例子中抛出的同一个文件中添加一个错误边界。

 export function ErrorBoundary ({ error }) { return ( < div className = ' bg-red-100 border border-red-300 p-4 ' > < h1 className = ' text-2xl ' > Something went wrong !< /h1 > < p > { error . message } < /p > < /div > ); }

现在刷新页面，看看会发生什么。

哇，错误只显示在特定的嵌套路由中！
我们应用程序的其他部分仍在按预期工作。

您可以在GitHub 上找到此示例代码。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

It's always annoying if your application throws unforeseen errors.

In general, it's good advice to build in the most common error catching early on. However, there might be some generic errors you can't always see coming.

Luckily for us, Remix will catch most of these errors and can render them to the closest ErrorBoundary box we define.

Creating a root error boundary

From my perspective, you'll always want to add a root error boundary to your code, this is the topmost level, so if an error is thrown top-level, this one will always be able to catch it.

To create one of these root error boundaries, open up the root.tsx file.

According to the docs, it's best to render a full HTML for the root error boundary as it will mount and unmount on the render of this error.
By having access to the <Meta />, <Links />, and <Script /> tags, it might be able to re-render.

Let's add the following function to this root file:

export function ErrorBoundary({ error }) { return ( <html> <head> <title>Oh no!</title>
 <Meta /> <Links /> </head>
 <body className='m-4'> <h1 className='text-2xl'>Something went wrong!</h1>
 <p>{error.message}</p>
 <Scripts /> </body>
 </html>
 );
}

Let's try it out and see what happens; I added this particular error in app/routes/admin/posts/index.tsx.

export function loader() { throw new Error('I am a failure!');
}

And if we now open this page, we get hit by the following error.

This is already way better than not throwing a custom error.
The downside is blocking the complete page while our error is only thrown down the line.

Adding nested error boundaries

And this is where Remix kind of blows my mind. It supports multiple error boundaries!

Remix will start looking for the closest error boundary to render the error when an error is thrown.

We could add an error boundary in the same file we throw it in in our case.

export function ErrorBoundary({ error }) { return ( <div className='bg-red-100 border border-red-300 p-4'> <h1 className='text-2xl'>Something went wrong!</h1>
 <p>{error.message}</p>
 </div>
 );
}

Now refresh the page and see what happens.

Wow, the error is only shown in the specific nested route!
The other parts of our application are still working as expected.

You can find this example code on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter
item.author: dailydevtips1

item.title: PPMT 功能 | PPMT Function
item.description:
公式

利率：（必填参数）- 每期的利率。

  • 类型：（可选参数）- 它指定付款是在期初还是期末进行。

    • 如何在 Excel 中使用 PPMT 函数

    示例：我们需要计算一笔 50,000 美元的贷款在第 1 个月和第 2 个月的本金还款额，该贷款将在 5 年后全额还清。 利息按每年 5% 的利率收取，并在每个月底偿还贷款。

    第 2 个月

    关于 PMT 功能的注意事项

    • 错误 - 发生在：

    给定速率值小于或等于 -1 给定 nper 值等于 0

    • 如果我们在计算月度或季度付款时忘记将利率或期间数转换为月或季度，则可能会出现错误。



    原文:

    PPMT FUNCTION Helps you get principal for given period.

    PPMT FUNCTION is a financial function, that calculates the payment on the principal for an investment based on periodic, constant payments and a fixed interest rate for a given period of time.

    Formula

    =PPMT(rate, per, nper, pv, [fv], [type])

    The PMT FUNCTION uses the following arguments:

    1. Rate:(required argument) - The interest rate per period.
    2. Per:(Required argument) - It is the bond's maturity date, that is, the date when bond expires.
    3. Nper:(required argument) - The total number of payments period in an annuity.
    4. Pv:(required argument) - The present value of the loan/investment.
    5. Fv:(optional argument) - Specifies the future value of the loan/investments.
    6. TYPE:(optional argument) - It specifies whether the payment is made at the start or the end of the period.

    How to use the PPMT Function in Excel

    Example: We need to calculate the payment on the principal for months 1 and 2 on a $50,000 loan, which is to be paid off in full after 5 years. Interest is charged at a rate of 5% per year and the loan repayments are to be made at the end of each month.

    For Month 2

    Things to remember about the PMT Function

    • #NUM! error - Occurs when:

    given rate value is less than or equal to -1 given nper value is equal to 0

    • An error can arise if we forget to convert the interest rate or the number of periods to months or quarters when calculating monthly or quarterly payments.
item.author: designegycreatives

item.title: 我在 Go fuzzing 上的最佳实践 | My best practices on Go fuzzing
item.description:
作为程序员，我们经常希望我们的代码没有错误！ 但事实是，无缺陷只能被证伪，不能被证实。 即将发布的 Go 1.18 正式提供了一个很好的工具来帮助我们证明它（在大多数情况下）—— go fuzzing 。

在本文中，我们将仔细研究go fuzzing:

  • 它是什么？
  • 如何使用 go fuzzing？
  • 最佳实践是什么？

什么是模糊测试

根据官方文档， go fuzzing是一种自动化测试的方法，它通过不断地为程序提供不同的输入并分析代码覆盖率来智能地发现失败的案例。 测试中发现的问题通常很难找到。

如何使用 go fuzzing

编写模糊测试的官方规则是。

  • 函数必须以 Fuzz 开头，唯一的参数是*testing.F ，并且没有返回值
  • 模糊测试必须在*_test.go文件中
  • 上图中的fuzz target是一个方法调用(*testing.F).Fuzz以*testing.T作为第一个参数，然后是称为fuzzing arguments的参数，没有返回值
  • 每个fuzz test只能有一个fuzz target
  • 调用f.Add(...)要求参数与fuzzing arguments具有相同的顺序和类型
  • 运行fuzzing tests

    如果我写一个fuzzing test ，例如

    // See https://github.com/zeromicro/go-zero/blob/master/core/mr/mapreduce_fuzz_test.go for the specific code func FuzzMapReduce ( f * testing . F ) { ... }

    然后我们可以这样执行。

    我们会得到类似下面的结果。

     fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 0/2 completed fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 2/2 completed, now fuzzing with 10 workers fuzz: elapsed: 3s, execs: 3338 ( 1112/sec ) , new interesting: 56 ( total: 57 ) fuzz: elapsed: 6s, execs: 6770 ( 1144/sec ) , new interesting: 62 ( total: 63 ) fuzz: elapsed: 9s, execs: 10157 ( 1129/sec ) , new interesting: 69 ( total: 70 ) fuzz: elapsed: 12s, execs: 13586 ( 1143/sec ) , new interesting: 72 ( total: 73 ) ^Cfuzz: elapsed: 13s, execs: 14031 ( 1084/sec ) , new interesting: 72 ( total: 73 ) PASS ok github.com/zeromicro/go-zero/core/mr 13.169s

    归零的最佳实践

    根据我在“归零”方面的经验，我将最佳实践总结为四个初步步骤。

    1. 定义fuzzing arguments ，首先弄清楚如何定义fuzzing arguments ，然后用给定的fuzzing arguments编写fuzzing target 。 想想怎么写fuzzing target ，这里的重点是如何验证结果的正确性，因为fuzzing arguments是“随机”给出的，所以应该有一个通用的方法来验证结果
    2. 想想如何打印失败案例的结果，这样就可以生成新的unit test
    3. 根据fuzzing test失败的结果编写一个新的unit test ，这个新的unit test将用于调试fuzzing test发现的问题，并为CI固化它们。

    下一步是用一个简单的数组求和函数来展示上述步骤。 这是求和代码的错误注入实现。

     func Sum ( vals [] int64 ) int64 { var total int64 for _ , val := range vals { if val % 1e5 ! = 0 { total += val } } return total }

    你需要至少给出一个fuzzing argument ，否则go fuzzing无法生成测试代码，所以即使我们没有好的输入，我们也需要定义一个会对结果产生影响的fuzzing argument ，这里我们使用将切片元素的数量作为fuzzing arguments ，然后Go fuzzing将根据运行的code coverage自动生成不同的参数来模拟测试。

     func FuzzSum ( f * testing . F ) { f . Add ( 10 ) f . Fuzz ( func ( t * testing . T , n int ) { n %= 20 ... }) }

    这里n是在切片中go fuzzing可以模拟的元素数量。 为了确保元素的数量不会太高，我们将其限制为 20（0 可以）并添加一个 10 的语料库（在go fuzzing中称为corpus ），这是使go fuzzing冷启动的值，它不会不管它是多少。

    这一步侧重于编写可验证的fuzzing target ，根据给定的fuzzing arguments编写测试代码，并生成数据以验证结果的正确性。

    对于我们的Sum函数，生成n元素的随机切片，然后对预期结果求和，其实是比较简单的。 如下。

     func FuzzSum ( f * testing . F ) { rand . Seed ( time . Now () . UnixNano ()) f . Add ( 10 ) f . Fuzz ( func ( t * testing . T , n int ) { n %= 20 var vals [] int64 var expect int64 for i := 0 ; i < n ; i ++ { val := rand . Int63 () % 1e6 vals = append ( vals , val ) expect += val } assert . Equal ( t , expect , Sum ( vals )) }) }

    这段代码还是很容易理解的，自己求和和Sum只做一个比较，就不详细解释了。 但是复杂的场景你需要仔细考虑如何编写验证码，但这不会太难，太难，可能对测试功能理解不够或简化。

    此时，您可以使用以下命令运行fuzzing tests ，结果将类似于以下内容。

     $ go test -fuzz = Sum fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 0/2 completed fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 2/2 completed, now fuzzing with 10 workers fuzz: elapsed: 0s, execs: 6672 ( 33646/sec ) , new interesting: 7 ( total: 6 ) --- FAIL: FuzzSum ( 0.21s ) --- FAIL: FuzzSum ( 0.00s ) sum_fuzz_test.go:34: Error Trace: sum_fuzz_test.go:34 value.go:556 value.go:339 fuzz.go:334 Error: Not equal: expected: 8736932 actual : 8636932 Test: FuzzSum Failing input written to testdata/fuzz/FuzzSum/739002313aceff0ff5ef993030bbde9115541cabee2554e6c9f3faaf581f2004 To re-run: go test -run = FuzzSum/739002313aceff0ff5ef993030bbde9115541cabee2554e6c9f3faaf581f2004 FAIL exit status 1 FAIL github.com/kevwan/fuzzing 0.614s

    所以这就是问题所在！ 我们看到结果不对，但是我们很难分析为什么不对，你仔细品味一下，上面这个输出，你是怎么分析的？

    对于上述失败的测试，如果我们可以将输入打印出来并形成一个简单的测试用例，那么我们就可以直接调试了。 最好是直接将打印的输入copy/paste到新的测试用例中，如果格式不对，需要逐行调整格式，这么多行输入太累了，可能不止一个失败案例。

    所以我们把代码改成如下。

     func FuzzSum ( f * testing . F ) { rand . Seed ( time . Now () . UnixNano ()) f . Add ( 10 ) f . Fuzz ( func ( t * testing . T , n int ) { n %= 20 var vals [] int64 var expect int64 var buf strings . buf . WriteString ( " \n " ) for i := 0 ; i < n ; i ++ { val := rand . Int63 () % 1e6 vals = append ( vals , val ) expect += val buf . WriteString ( fmt . Sprintf ( "%d, \n " , val )) } assert . Equal ( t , expect , Sum ( vals ), buf . String ()) }) }

    再次运行该命令会得到以下结果。

     $ go test -fuzz = Sum fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 0/2 completed fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 2/2 completed, now fuzzing with 10 workers fuzz: elapsed: 0s, execs: 1402 ( 10028/sec ) , new interesting: 10 ( total: 8 ) --- FAIL: FuzzSum ( 0.16s ) --- FAIL: FuzzSum ( 0.00s ) sum_fuzz_test.go:34: Error Trace: sum_fuzz_test.go:34 value.go:556 value.go:339 fuzz.go:334 Error: Not equal: expected: 5823336 actual : 5623336 Test: FuzzSum Messages: 799023, 110387, 811082, 115543, 859422, 997646, 200000, 399008, 7905, 931332, 591988, Failing input written to testdata/fuzz/FuzzSum/26d024acf85aae88f3291bf7e1c6f473eab8b051f2adb1bf05d4491bc49f5767 To re-run: go test -run = FuzzSum/26d024acf85aae88f3291bf7e1c6f473eab8b051f2adb1bf05d4491bc49f5767 FAIL exit status 1 FAIL github.com/kevwan/fuzzing 0.602s

    根据上面失败案例的输出，我们可以copy/paste下面的代码，当然框架是自己写的，输入参数可以直接复制进去。

     func TestSumFuzzCase1 ( t * testing . T ) { vals := [] int64 { 799023 , 110387 , 811082 , 115543 , 859422 , 997646 , 200000 , 399008 , 7905 , 931332 , 591988 , } assert . Equal ( t , int64 ( 5823336 ), Sum ( vals )) }

    这使得调试和添加有效的unit test变得容易，以确保错误不再出现。

    相信Go 1.18已经发布了，大部分项目都不会马上将自己的在线代码升级到1.18，那么如果go fuzzing引入的testing.F不起作用怎么办？

    如果线上（go.mod）没有升级到Go 1.18，但是我们完全推荐在本地升级，那么我们只需要将上面的FuzzSum放到一个文件名为sum_fuzz_test.go的文件中，并在标题。

     //go:build go1.18 // +build go1.18

    注意：第三行必须是空行，否则会变成package注释。

    这样无论我们使用哪个版本我们都不会在线报告错误，并且我们通常在本地运行fuzz testing并且不受影响。

    上面的步骤是针对简单的情况，但有时当来自失败案例的输入形成一个新的unit test不能重现问题时（特别是如果有 goroutine 死锁问题），问题会变得复杂，如下所示输出。

     go test -fuzz = MapReduce fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 0/2 completed fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 2/2 completed, now fuzzing with 10 workers fuzz: elapsed: 3s, execs: 3681 ( 1227/sec ) , new interesting: 54 ( total: 55 ) ... fuzz: elapsed: 1m21s, execs: 92705 ( 1101/sec ) , new interesting: 85 ( total: 86 ) --- FAIL: FuzzMapReduce ( 80.96s ) fuzzing process hung or terminated unexpectedly: exit status 2 Failing input written to testdata/fuzz/FuzzMapReduce/ee6a61e8c968adad2e629fba11984532cac5d177c4899d3e0b7c2949a0a3d840 To re-run: go test -run = FuzzMapReduce/ee6a61e8c968adad2e629fba11984532cac5d177c4899d3e0b7c2949a0a3d840 FAIL exit status 1 FAIL github.com/zeromicro/go-zero/core/mr 81.471s

    在这种情况下，它只是告诉我们fuzzing process被卡住或异常结束，状态码为 2。在这种情况下， re-run无法正常重现。 为什么它只是返回错误代码 2？ 我浏览了go fuzzing的源代码，每个fuzzing test都由一个单独的进程运行，然后go fuzzing丢弃了 fuzzing 测试的进程输出，只显示状态码。 那么我们如何解决这个问题呢？

    经过仔细分析，我决定自己编写一个像fuzzing test这样的常规单元测试代码，这样可以确保失败在同一个进程中，并将错误信息打印到标准输出，代码大致如下。

     func TestSumFuzzRandom ( t * testing . T ) { const times = 100000 rand . Seed ( time . Now () . UnixNano ()) for i := 0 ; i < times ; i ++ { n := rand . Intn ( 20 ) var vals [] int64 var expect int64 var buf strings . buf . WriteString ( " \n " ) for i := 0 ; i < n ; i ++ { val := rand . Int63 () % 1e6 vals = append ( vals , val ) expect += val buf . WriteString ( fmt . Sprintf ( "%d, \n " , val )) } assert . Equal ( t , expect , Sum ( vals ), buf . String ()) } }

    这样我们就可以自己做一个简单的go fuzzing模拟，但如果出现任何错误，我们都可以获得清晰的输出。 这里也许我们通过go fuzzing来学习，或者有其他的方法来控制它，如果你知道，谢谢你告诉我。

    但是这个模拟案例需要很长时间才能运行，我们不希望它每次都在 CI 执行，所以我把它放在一个单独的文件中，命名为sum_fuzzcase_test.go并将以下指令添加到标题文件。

     //go:build fuzz // +build fuzz

    这样我们可以在需要运行这个模拟案例时添加-tags fuzz ，例如

    go test -tags fuzz . /...

    复杂的使用示例

    上面是一个例子，还是比较简单的，如果遇到复杂的场景不知道怎么写，可以先看看 go-zero 是如何落地go fuzzing的，如下。

    项目地址

    随意使用go-zero和star来支持我们！



原文:

As programmers, we often hope that our code is bug-free! But the fact is that bug-free can only be disproved, not proven. The upcoming release of Go 1.18 officially provides a great tool to help us prove it (in most cases) - go fuzzing.

Go 1.18 is all about generics, but I really think go fuzzing is the most useful feature of Go 1.18 at the moment, even compared with generics!

In this article, we'll take a closer look at go fuzzing:

  • What is it?
  • How to use go fuzzing?
  • What are the best practices?

What is go fuzzing

According to the official documentation, go fuzzing is a way to automate tests by continuously giving different inputs to a program and analyzing the code coverage to intelligently find the failed cases. The problems found in the test are usually difficult to find.

How to use go fuzzing

The official rules for writing fuzz tests are.

  • The function must start with Fuzz, the only argument is *testing.F, and there is no return value
  • Fuzz tests must be in the *_test.go file
  • The fuzz target in the above image is a method call (*testing.F).Fuzz with *testing.T as the first argument, and then arguments called fuzzing arguments, with no return value
  • There can be only one fuzz target in each fuzz test
  • Calling f.Add(...) requires that the arguments be of the same order and type as the fuzzing arguments
  • fuzzing arguments only supports the following types.
    • string, []byte
    • int, int8, int16, int32/rune, int64
    • uint, uint8/byte, uint16, uint32, uint64
    • float32, float64
    • bool
  • fuzz target Do not rely on global state, it will run in parallel.

Run fuzzing tests

If I write a fuzzing test, e.g.

// See https://github.com/zeromicro/go-zero/blob/master/core/mr/mapreduce_fuzz_test.go for the specific code
func FuzzMapReduce(f *testing.F) { ...
}

Then we can execute it like this.

We would get something like the following result.

fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 0/2 completed
fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 2/2 completed, now fuzzing with 10 workers
fuzz: elapsed: 3s, execs: 3338 (1112/sec), new interesting: 56 (total: 57)
fuzz: elapsed: 6s, execs: 6770 (1144/sec), new interesting: 62 (total: 63)
fuzz: elapsed: 9s, execs: 10157 (1129/sec), new interesting: 69 (total: 70)
fuzz: elapsed: 12s, execs: 13586 (1143/sec), new interesting: 72 (total: 73)
^Cfuzz: elapsed: 13s, execs: 14031 (1084/sec), new interesting: 72 (total: 73)
PASS
ok github.com/zeromicro/go-zero/core/mr 13.169s

The ^C is because I pressed Ctrl-C to terminate the test, see the official documentation for details.

best practices in go-zero

Based on my experience with go-zero, I've summarized the best practices in four preliminary steps.

  1. define fuzzing arguments, first figure out how to define fuzzing arguments, and write fuzzing target with the given fuzzing arguments. 2.
  2. think about how to write fuzzing target, the focus here is on how to verify the correctness of the results, because fuzzing arguments are given "randomly", so there should be a general method to verify the results
  3. think about how to print the result of a failed case, so that a new unit test can be generated
  4. write a new unit test based on the results of the failed fuzzing test, this new unit test will be used to debug the problems found by the fuzzing test and solidify them for the CI.

The next step is to show the above steps with a simple array summation function. The actual case in go-zero is slightly more complicated, and I will give the internal go-zero landing case at the end of the article for you to write complex scenarios.

Here is a bug-injected implementation of the summation code.

func Sum(vals []int64) int64 { var total int64 for _, val := range vals { if val%1e5 ! = 0 { total += val } } return total
}

1. Define fuzzing arguments

You need to give at least one fuzzing argument, otherwise go fuzzing can't generate test code, so even if we don't have good input, we need to define a fuzzing argument that will have an impact on the result, here we use the number of slice elements as fuzzing arguments, and then Go fuzzing will automatically generate different arguments to simulate the test based on the code coverage that is run.

func FuzzSum(f *testing.F) { f.Add(10) f.Fuzz(func(t *testing.T, n int) { n %= 20 ... })
}

Here n is the number of elements that go fuzzing can simulate in a slice. To make sure the number of elements is not too high, we limit it to 20 (0 is fine) and we add a corpus of 10 (called corpus in go fuzzing), which is the value that makes go fuzzing cold start, it doesn't matter how much it is.

2. How to write the fuzzing target

This step focuses on writing a verifiable fuzzing target, writing test code based on the given fuzzing arguments, and generating data to verify the correctness of the results.

For our Sum function, it is actually relatively simple to generate a random slice of n elements and then sum up the expected result. As follows.

func FuzzSum(f *testing.F) { rand.Seed(time.Now().UnixNano()) f.Add(10) f.Fuzz(func(t *testing.T, n int) { n %= 20 var vals []int64 var expect int64 for i := 0; i < n; i++ { val := rand.Int63() % 1e6 vals = append(vals, val) expect += val } assert.Equal(t, expect, Sum(vals)) })
}

This code is still very easy to understand, their own summation and Sum summation to do a comparison only, will not explain in detail. But complex scenarios you need to think carefully about how to write the verification code, but this will not be too difficult, too difficult, it may not be enough understanding of the test function or simplify.

At this point, you can run fuzzing tests with the following command, and the result will be similar to the following.

$ go test -fuzz=Sum
fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 0/2 completed
fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 2/2 completed, now fuzzing with 10 workers
fuzz: elapsed: 0s, execs: 6672 (33646/sec), new interesting: 7 (total: 6)
--- FAIL: FuzzSum (0.21s) --- FAIL: FuzzSum (0.00s) sum_fuzz_test.go:34: Error Trace: sum_fuzz_test.go:34 value.go:556 value.go:339 fuzz.go:334 Error: Not equal: expected: 8736932 actual : 8636932 Test: FuzzSum Failing input written to testdata/fuzz/FuzzSum/739002313aceff0ff5ef993030bbde9115541cabee2554e6c9f3faaf581f2004 To re-run: go test -run=FuzzSum/739002313aceff0ff5ef993030bbde9115541cabee2554e6c9f3faaf581f2004
FAIL
exit status 1
FAIL github.com/kevwan/fuzzing 0.614s

So here's the problem! We see the result is not right, but we can hardly analyze why it is not right, you taste carefully, this output above, how do you analyze?

3. How to print the input for the failed case

For the above failed test, if we can print out the input and form a simple test case, then we can debug it directly. It is better to copy/paste the printed input directly to the new test case, if the format is not right, you need to adjust the format line by line for so many lines of input is too tired, and it may not be only one failure case.

So we changed the code to the following.

func FuzzSum(f *testing.F) { rand.Seed(time.Now().UnixNano()) f.Add(10) f.Fuzz(func(t *testing.T, n int) { n %= 20 var vals []int64 var expect int64 var buf strings. buf.WriteString("\n") for i := 0; i < n; i++ { val := rand.Int63() % 1e6 vals = append(vals, val) expect += val buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%d,\n", val)) } assert.Equal(t, expect, Sum(vals), buf.String()) })
}

Running the command again gives the following result.

$ go test -fuzz=Sum
fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 0/2 completed
fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 2/2 completed, now fuzzing with 10 workers
fuzz: elapsed: 0s, execs: 1402 (10028/sec), new interesting: 10 (total: 8)
--- FAIL: FuzzSum (0.16s) --- FAIL: FuzzSum (0.00s) sum_fuzz_test.go:34: Error Trace: sum_fuzz_test.go:34 value.go:556 value.go:339 fuzz.go:334 Error: Not equal: expected: 5823336 actual : 5623336 Test: FuzzSum Messages: 799023, 110387, 811082, 115543, 859422, 997646, 200000, 399008, 7905, 931332, 591988, Failing input written to testdata/fuzz/FuzzSum/26d024acf85aae88f3291bf7e1c6f473eab8b051f2adb1bf05d4491bc49f5767 To re-run: go test -run=FuzzSum/26d024acf85aae88f3291bf7e1c6f473eab8b051f2adb1bf05d4491bc49f5767
FAIL
exit status 1
FAIL github.com/kevwan/fuzzing 0.602s

4. Write a new test case

Based on the output of the failure case above, we can copy/paste the following code, but of course the framework is written by ourselves and the input parameters can be copied in directly.

func TestSumFuzzCase1(t *testing.T) { vals := []int64{ 799023, 110387, 811082, 115543, 859422, 997646, 200000, 399008, 7905, 931332, 591988, } assert.Equal(t, int64(5823336), Sum(vals))
}

This makes it easy to debug and to add a valid unit test to ensure that the bug never comes up again.

go fuzzing more experience

Go versioning issues

I believe that Go 1.18 has been released, and most projects will not immediately upgrade their online code to 1.18, so what if the testing.F introduced by go fuzzing doesn't work?

If the online (go.mod) is not upgraded to Go 1.18, but we are fully recommended to upgrade locally, then we just need to put the above FuzzSum into a file with a name like sum_fuzz_test.go and add the following directive to the header.

//go:build go1.18
// +build go1.18

Note: The third line must be a blank line, otherwise it will become a package comment.

This way we don't report errors online no matter which version we use, and we run fuzz testing usually locally and are not affected.

go fuzzing does not reproduce failures

The above steps are for simple cases, but sometimes the problem becomes complicated when the input from the failure case forms a new unit test that does not reproduce the problem (especially if there is a goroutine deadlock problem), as you can see in the following output.

go test -fuzz=MapReduce
fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 0/2 completed
fuzz: elapsed: 0s, gathering baseline coverage: 2/2 completed, now fuzzing with 10 workers
fuzz: elapsed: 3s, execs: 3681 (1227/sec), new interesting: 54 (total: 55)
...
fuzz: elapsed: 1m21s, execs: 92705 (1101/sec), new interesting: 85 (total: 86)
--- FAIL: FuzzMapReduce (80.96s) fuzzing process hung or terminated unexpectedly: exit status 2 Failing input written to testdata/fuzz/FuzzMapReduce/ee6a61e8c968adad2e629fba11984532cac5d177c4899d3e0b7c2949a0a3d840 To re-run: go test -run=FuzzMapReduce/ee6a61e8c968adad2e629fba11984532cac5d177c4899d3e0b7c2949a0a3d840
FAIL
exit status 1
FAIL github.com/zeromicro/go-zero/core/mr 81.471s

In this case, it just tells us that the fuzzing process is stuck or has ended abnormally, with a status code of 2. In this case, the re-run is not normally reproduced. Why does it simply return an error code of 2? I went through the source code of go fuzzing, and each fuzzing test is run by a separate process, and then go fuzzing throws away the process output of the fuzzing test and just shows the status code. So how do we solve this problem?

After careful analysis, I decided to write a regular unit test code like fuzzing test myself, which would ensure that the failures are in the same process and would print the error message to the standard output, the code is roughly as follows.

func TestSumFuzzRandom(t *testing.T) { const times = 100000 rand.Seed(time.Now().UnixNano()) for i := 0; i < times; i++ { n := rand.Intn(20) var vals []int64 var expect int64 var buf strings. buf.WriteString("\n") for i := 0; i < n; i++ { val := rand.Int63() % 1e6 vals = append(vals, val) expect += val buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%d,\n", val)) } assert.Equal(t, expect, Sum(vals), buf.String()) }
}

This way we can do a simple simulation of go fuzzing ourselves, but with any errors we can get clear output. Here maybe we study through go fuzzing, or there are other ways to control it, if you know, thanks for letting me know.

But this simulated case that takes a long time to run, we wouldn't want it to be executed every time at CI, so I put it in a separate file named something like sum_fuzzcase_test.go and added the following directive to the header of the file.

//go:build fuzz
// +build fuzz

This way we can add -tags fuzz when we need to run this mock case, e.g.

go test -tags fuzz . /...

Complex usage examples

The above is an example, still relatively simple, if you encounter complex scenarios do not know how to write, you can first see how go-zero is landed go fuzzing, as follows.

Project address

https://github.com/zeromicro/go-zero

Feel free to use go-zero and star to support us!
item.author: kevwan

item.title: 如何将 python-flask 应用程序部署到 Heroku。 避免 [H10-Error] ！ | How To Deploy A python-flask app to Heroku. Avoid the [H10-Error] !
item.description:
阿蒂诺·奥万达

发表于将烧瓶应用程序部署到 Heroku 可能会变得很麻烦！ 即使您以正确的方式完成了所有操作，著名的 H10 Heroku 错误也会突然出现！

这是一种使部署变得不那么忙碌而更成功的方法！

  1. 创建一个 Procfile，然后添加： < web: gunicorn manage:app > 在这种情况下， app 是您的应用程序名称， manage 是您用于运行代码的文件

  2. 登录您的 Heroku 帐户或创建一个新帐户：< heroku login >

  3. 接下来创建应用程序：heroku create < name-of-app >

  4. 如果你有 API 或密钥>> heroku config:set API_KEY= < your-api-key >

  5. 如果您还没有安装以下（虚拟）：（请记住使用 pip install）：

  • 烧瓶 1.1.4
  • 独角兽
  • 参数完成 2.0.0
  • 标记安全 2.0.1
  • 使用以下命令生成requirements.txt ： pip freeze > requirements.txt

  • 在项目根文件夹文件中创建一个runtime.txt ，然后添加您正在使用的 python 版本：例如： python-3.9.12

  • 在终端上，运行heroku config:set DISABLE_COLLECTSTATIC=1

    • 请记住：修复前一个错误后的新错误是进步……黑客快乐！

纳尔逊·阿多尼斯·埃尔南德斯 - 赛斯迈克尔拉尔森 - 周翠珊👩🏻‍💻 - Aug 1 '21 21 年 8 月 1 日



原文:

Atieno Obwanda

Posted on May 3

Deploying a flask app to Heroku can somehow become a hustle! Even when you have done everything the right way,the famous H10 Heroku error can just pop up out of nowhere!

Here is a way that makes deployment less of a hustle and more of a win!

  1. - Navigate to your config file,config.py, ensure that the DEBUG = True is in the same line as class DevConfig(Config): 
 class DevConfig(Config):

 DEBUG = True

  1. Create a Procfile, then add: < web: gunicorn manage:app> In this case, app is your application name and manage is the file you're using to run the code

  2. Login to your Heroku account or create a new one:< heroku login >

  3. Next create the app: heroku create <name-of-app>

  4. If you have API or secret key>> heroku config:set API_KEY= <your-api-key>

  5. Install the following(in virtual) if you haven’t: (remember to use pip install):

  • Flask 1.1.4
  • Gunicorn
  • Argcomplete 2.0.0
  • MarkUpsafe 2.0.1
  • Django-cors-header 3.11.0

  1. Generate a requirements.txt using: pip freeze > requirements.txt

  2. Create a runtime.txt in the project root folder file and add then python version you’re using: For example: python-3.9.12

  3. On terminal, run heroku config:set DISABLE_COLLECTSTATIC=1

--->Lastly, 1.Git add . =>2. Git Commit -m ”deployment updates” => 3.Git push heroku main/master(depending on your branch name)

Remember: A new bug after fixing the previous one is progress… Happy Hacking!

Nelson Adonis Hernández - Aug 3 '21

Seth Michael Larson - Aug 2 '21

Tracy Chou 👩🏻‍💻 - Aug 1 '21
item.author: atienoobwanda

item.title: 工程指标基准：是什么造就了精英团队？ | Engineering Metrics Benchmarks: What Makes Elite Teams?
item.description:
六个月前，LinearB 的数据科学团队决定从 DORA 停止的地方继续，比以往任何时候都更深入地挖掘数据。 历史上第一次，工程团队能够根据数据支持的行业标准对他们的性能进行基准测试。

我们的研究分析了：

  • 根据这些指标对组织的绩效进行基准测试是了解工程运营效率和有效性的好方法。 开始您的旅程的最佳方式是收集数据。

    为了帮助您为您的团队生成这九个指标， LinearB 为开发团队提供免费帐户。 我们的平台将来自您的 GitHub、GitLab 或 Bitbucket 存储库的数据与来自您的项目管理工具（如 Jira）的数据相关联，以尽可能准确地了解您的团队绩效。

    在本文档的其余部分，我们将详细介绍我们如何计算每个指标、我们做出这些选择的原因以及如何使用每个指标来改进您的工程组织。

    计算工程指标基准

    虽然 DORA Metrics 和 Accelerate 这本书基于访谈和评估，但 Engineering Metrics Benchmarks 仅基于来自工作开发团队的数据。 数据本身来自 LinearB 平台的成千上万的免费和付费用户。 我们的数据是值得信赖的，因为它来自全球工程师群体，他们正在以各种方式改进他们的工程指标。 由于 LinearB 平台足够灵活，允许用户通过 Git、项目管理、API 或所有三者跟踪他们的工作，我们的整体数据集更加多样化，因此可以更好地代表工程团队在现实生活中的工作方式。

    当我们开始研究时，我们必须决定哪些数据最适合为我们提供可信的结果。 所以我们从列一个清单开始：

    • 我们不会取出异常值
    • 我们只会衡量合并的 PR
    • 仅包括拥有至少 400 个分支机构的组织
    • 我们将仅包括拥有 270 天数据的组织
    • 我们将从我们的计算中排除草稿 PR 和排除的分支

    应用这些规则后，我们首先获取 270 天期间的平均指标值，并开始将它们绘制在图表上。 完成此操作后，我们就可以将百分位数纳入我们的排名。

    • 前 10% – 精英
    • 最后 40% – 需要关注

    为什么我们使用这些百分位数？ 这是我们在研究过程中遇到的一个问题。 为什么不让精英进入前 20%，让每个人都对自己的表现感觉好一点呢？ 我们是否应该让公平和强大一点点更大，以便更多的组织落在图表的中间？

    归根结底，我们想表达我们认为最符合数据精神的东西。 在这方面，这意味着精英应该是真正的特别。 组织应该关注任何属于最后 40% 的指标，以了解如何改进它。

    工程指标

    图表中显示的九个指标都是工程组织质量和效率水平的指标。 使用这些指标来确定重点领域、设定目标并采取措施改善业务价值交付的团队。

    周期：

    衡量从首次提交到生产发布的时间量。 它是从精益制造中借鉴的指标，也是软件开发团队最重要的指标之一。

    短周期时间表明组织正在以更快的速度向客户交付价值。 较短的周期时间与较小的 PR 规模、健康的审核流程和较高的部署频率相关。 总体而言，具有精英周期时间的团队能够以更高的质量交付更多的功能。

    长周期时间是由多种原因造成的，最常见的是公关审查过程中的瓶颈。 更长的周期时间会降低团队效率以及可预测地交付功能的能力。

    编码时间：

    测量从第一次提交到发出拉取请求所花费的时间。

    短编码时间与低 WIP、小 PR 尺寸和明确要求相关。 综合起来，这些好处意味着开发人员上下文切换成本（即重新关注任务所花费的时间）的减少。

    较长的编码时间与较大的 PR 规模、较长的 PR 审查时间和代码质量的降低相关。 当需求不明确或工作没有分解成小块时，代码会变得更加复杂，并且在生产前彻底审查更具挑战性。

    取货时间：

    衡量一个拉取请求等待某人开始审查它的时间。

    取件时间短代表了强大的团队合作和健康的审核流程。 当一个 PR 在 24 小时内被提取和审查时，代码在开发人员的脑海中仍然是新鲜的。 这种认知负荷的减少节省了大量的重新聚焦时间。

    取件时间长表示常见的工作流程瓶颈。 要么团队不知道 PR 已准备好进行审查，要么没有足够的开发人员进行审查。 在这两种情况下，切换之间较长的过渡时间与较高的认知负荷（重新聚焦时间）和效率降低相关。

    审核时间：

    衡量完成代码审查和合并拉取请求所需的时间。

    低审查时间与较小的 PR 大小、降低的代码复杂性和较短的周期时间相关。 这里需要注意的是确保您的评论深度（每条评论的评论数量）在 2 到 4 之间保持一致。

    审核时间长表示 PR 规模大、缺乏关注时间和/或质量问题。 由于开发人员需要“重新投入”任务，大型​​ PR 审查期间的定期中断会增加审查时间。 类似地，PR 审查花费的时间越长，代码在开发者心中的新鲜感就越少。 高返工率也与长审查时间相关。

    部署时间：

    测量从合并分支到发布代码的时间。

    低部署时间与高部署频率和更稳定的生产环境相关。 高部署时间与人工工作和低代码质量相关。 通过标准化小型 PR 规模和彻底的 PR 审查流程，可以减少构建失败。

    返工率：

    衡量对不到 21 天的代码所做的更改量。

    低返工率表明更高的整体代码质量和强大的 PR 审查流程。

    高返工率信号代码流失，是质量问题的主要指标。

    规划精度：

    衡量计划工作与冲刺或迭代期间实际交付的工作的比率。

    高计划准确度表示高水平的可预测性。 低计划准确度表示计划外工作、影子工作和高代码流失率。 错过交货期限和高残留是计划准确性低的最常见结果。


    使用 LinearB 的 Project Delivery Tracker 可以帮助您可视化您的团队在每个 sprint 中进行的计划外工作，并提高您的计划准确性。 立即预订演示！

    部署频率：

    衡量代码发布的频率。

    高部署频率代表了一个稳定和健康的持续交付管道。 精英开发团队每天多次将小块代码部署到生产环境中，以改善用户体验并缩短反馈循环。

    低部署频率是大型部署事件的一个指标，会增加生产不稳定的可能性。

    公关尺寸：

    测量拉取请求中修改的代码行数。

    较小的 PR 尺寸更容易审查、更安全地合并，并且与更短的周期时间相关。

    大 PR 大小直接与更高的周期时间和代码复杂性相关。 高 PR 尺寸是高周期时间的最常见原因。

    了解团队当前的表现是创建持续改进文化的第一步。 如果您准备好了解您的团队在当今行业标准的表现如何，请使用此链接预订免费的工程指标基准咨询。



原文:

In 2014 the DevOps Research and Assessment (DORA) team published their first State of DevOps report, identifying four metrics that can be used to measure engineering team performance.

Six months ago the Data Science Team at LinearB decided to continue where DORA left off, digging deeper into the data than ever before. For the first time in history, engineering teams are able to benchmark their performance against data-backed industry standards.

Our study analyzed:

  • 1,971 Engineering Teams
  • 847K Branches


The Engineering Metrics Benchmarks chart was created from a study of 1,971 dev teams, and 847k branches over a four-month period.

Benchmarking your organization’s performance against these metrics is a great way to understand the efficiency and effectiveness of your engineering operation. The best way to begin your journey is by gathering data.

To help you generate these nine metrics for your team, LinearB provides free accounts for dev teams. Our platform correlates data from your GitHub, GitLab or Bitbucket repos with data from your project management tool, like Jira, to provide the most accurate picture of your team’s performance possible.

Throughout the rest of this document, we will be providing details into how we calculated each metric, why we made the choices we did, and how each metric can be used to improve your engineering organization.

Calculating Engineering Metric Benchmarks

While the DORA Metrics and Accelerate book are based on interviews and assessments, the Engineering Metrics Benchmarks are based solely on data from working dev teams. The data itself comes from the many thousands of free and paid users of the LinearB platform. Our data is trusted because it comes from a global population of engineers who are improving their engineering metrics in a variety of ways. Since the LinearB platform is flexible enough to allow users to track their work through Git, project management, API or all three, our overall data sets are more diverse and therefore a better representation of how engineering teams work in real life.

When we began our study, we had to decide on what data was best suited to provide us with trusted results. So we started by making a list:

  • We will not take out outliers
  • We will only measure Merged PRs
  • Will only include organizations with at least 400 branches
  • We will only include organizations that had 270 days worth of data
  • We would exclude draft PRs and excluded branches from our calculation

With these rules applied, we started by taking the average metric value during a 270 day period and began plotting them on a graph. Once this was done, we were able to take the percentiles that would turn into our rankings.

  • Top 10% – Elite
  • 11 – 30 % – Strong
  • 31 – 60% – Fair
  • Last 40% – Needs Focus

Why did we use these percentiles? It was a question we struggled with during the study. Why not make Elite the top 20% and make everyone feel a bit better about their performance? Should we make Fair and Strong a little bit larger so more organizations land in the middle of the chart?

At the end of the day, we wanted to represent what we felt was most true to the spirit of the data. In this regard, that meant Elite should be truly special. And any metric that falls into the last forty percent should be focused on by the organizations to understand how it can be improved.

Engineering Metrics

The nine metrics shown in the chart are all indicators of an engineering organization’s level of quality and efficiency. Teams who use these metrics to identify areas for focus, set goals and take steps to improve significantly increase their business value delivery.

Cycle Time:

Measures the amount of time from first commit to production release. It is a metric borrowed from lean manufacturing, and it is one of the most important metrics for software development teams.

Short Cycle Time demonstrates that an organization is delivering value to customers at a faster rate. Short Cycle Time correlates to small PR sizes, a healthy review process, and high deployment frequency. Overall, teams with an elite Cycle Time are able to deliver more features predictably and at a higher quality.

Long Cycle Time is caused by multiple reasons, the most common being bottlenecks within the PR review process. Longer Cycle Times reduce team efficiency as well as the ability to deliver features predictably.

Coding Time:

measures the time it takes from the first commit until a pull request is issued.

Short Coding Time correlates to low WIP, small PR size and clear requirements. Combined, these benefits mean a reduction in developer context switching costs (i.e. time spent re-focusing on a task).

Long Coding Time correlates to larger PR sizes, longer PR Review Times and a reduction in code quality. When requirements are unclear or work isn’t broken down into small chunks, code becomes more complex and more challenging to review thoroughly before production.

Pickup Time:

Measures the time a pull request waits for someone to start reviewing it.

Low Pickup Time represents strong teamwork and a healthy review process. When a PR is picked up and reviewed within 24 hours, the code is still fresh in the mind of the developer. This reduction of cognitive load saves a significant amount of refocus time.

Long Pickup Time is an indication of a common workflow bottleneck. Either the team isn’t aware a PR is ready for review or there aren’t enough developers taking on reviews. In both instances, a longer transition time between handoffs correlates to higher cognitive load (re-focus time) and reduced efficiency.

Review Time:

Measures the time it takes to complete a code review and get a pull request merged.

Low Review Time correlates to small PR sizes, reduced code complexity and short Cycle Time. The caveat here is to make sure your Review Depth (the number of comments per review) is consistent between two and four.

Long Review Time indicates large PR sizes, a lack of focus time and/or quality issues. Regular interruptions during a large PR review increase Review Time due to the developer needing to “get back into” the task. In a similar light, the longer a PR review takes, the less fresh the code will be in the mind of the owning developer. High rework rates also correlate to long review times.

Deploy Time:

Measures the time from when a branch is merged to when the code is released.

Low Deploy Time correlates to high deployment frequency and a more stable production environment. Modern development practices like CI/CD and automated testing improve the time to production and the feedback loop from users.

High Deploy Time correlates to manual efforts and low code quality. Build failures can be reduced by standardizing small PR sizes and a thorough PR review process.

Rework Rate:

Measures the amount of changes made to code that is less than 21 days old.

Low Rework Rates are an indication of higher overall code quality and a strong PR review process.

High Rework Rates signal code churn and is a leading indicator of quality issues.

Planning Accuracy:

Measures the ratio of planned work vs. what is actually delivered during a sprint or iteration.

High Planning Accuracy indicates a high level of predictability. Predictability within a sprint or larger project allows teams to deliver what they promised to deliver, on time.

Low Planning Accuracy is an indication of unplanned work, shadow work and high code churn. Missed delivery deadlines and high carryover are the most common result of low planning accuracy.


Using LinearB’s Project Delivery Tracker can help you visualize unplanned work your team is doing each sprint and improve your planning accuracy. Book a demo today!

Deployment Frequency:

Measures how often code is released.

High Deployment Frequency represents a stable and healthy continuous delivery pipeline. Elite development teams are deploying small chunks of code to production multiple times a day to improve the user experience and shorten the feedback loop.

Low Deployment Frequency is an indicator of large deployment events that increase the likelihood of production instability.

PR Size:

Measures the number of code lines modified in a pull request.

Small PR Sizes are easier to review, safer to merge, and correlate to a lower Cycle Time.

Large PR Sizes directly correlate to higher Cycle Time and code complexity. High PR sizes are the most common cause of a high Cycle Time.

Understanding your team’s current performance is the first step to creating a culture of continuous improvement. If you’re ready to discover how your team performs against industry standards today, use this link to book a free engineering metrics benchmarks consultation.

item.title: 2022 年面向前端开发人员的顶级 VS 代码扩展 | Top VS Code Extensions for Front-End Developers in 2022
item.description:
前端开发人员，如果您使用 VS Code 作为开发的 IDE，我们有一些很棒的扩展可以让您作为程序员的日常生活更轻松。

这是我们最喜欢的 VS Code 扩展列表，我们发现它们非常有用。

它通过解析您的代码并使用自己的规则重新打印它来执行一致的样式，这些规则考虑了最大行长度，并在必要时包装代码。

项目仪表板

将您经常访问的文件夹、文件和 SSH 远程固定到仪表板上以快速访问它们。

这是 VS Code 的扩展，可以自动为 React 组件生成 PropTypes 代码，例如Jetbrains 平台中的ReactPropTypes 。 也是一个命令行工具，你不必局限于在 vs 代码中使用。 如果你想全自动生成 PropTypes，你也可以在你的项目中将它与 lint-staged 一起使用。

用于 VS Code 的智能 Tailwind CSS 工具。

自动化编写有意义的日志消息的过程。

重构你杂草丛生的 JSX，而不用担心给定的数据。

包 Json 升级

在 package.json 中显示可用的更新。 提供快速操作来指导您进行更新。

用于 Python、JavaScript、TypeScript 的 Mintlify 文档编写器，
适用于 JavaScript、Python、Java、Typescript 和所有其他语言的人工智能文档编写器。

材质图标主题

进口成本

在编辑器中显示导入/要求包大小。

图像预览

在装订线和悬停时显示图像预览。

查看存储库的 Git 图表，并从图表中执行 Git 操作。

颜色亮点

在编辑器中突出显示网页颜色。

改变案例

快速更改当前选择或当前单词的大小写（camelCase、CONSTANT_CASE、snake_case 等）。

更好的评论

通过使用警报、信息、TODO 等进行注释来改进您的代码注释！

自动重命名标签

自动重命名成对的 HTML/XML 标签。

安卓 iOS 模拟器

如果你是移动开发者，从 vs code 轻松运行 Android 模拟器和 iOS 模拟器！

感谢您一直阅读。

希望这篇文章对改善您的开发者之旅有用！

原文发表于： https ://blogs.appymango.com/blogs/62703ae8483b05951950af41



原文:

Front-End Developers, if you are using VS Code as your IDE for your development, we have some great extensions to make your day-to-day life as programmers easier.

Here is the list of our favorite VS Code extensions, which we found to be incredibly useful.

Prettier - Code formatter

Prettier is an opinionated code formatter. It enforces a consistent style by parsing your code and re-printing it with its own rules that take the maximum line length into account, wrapping code when necessary.

Project Dashboard

VSCode Project Dashboard is a Visual Studio Code extension that lets you organize your projects in a speed-dial like manner. Pin your frequently visited folders, files, and SSH remotes onto a dashboard to access them quickly.

React PropTypes Generate

This is the VS Code's extension that automatically generates PropTypes code for React components, like ReactPropTypes in the Jetbrains's Platform. Also a command-line tool, you don’t have to be limited to use in vs code. If you want fully automatic PropTypes generation, You can also use it with lint-staged in your project.

Tailwind CSS IntelliSense

Intelligent Tailwind CSS tooling for VS Code.

Turbo Console Log

Automating the process of writing meaningful log messages.

VSCode React Refactor

Recompose your overgrown JSX without worrying about the given data.

Package Json Upgrade

Shows available updates in package.json. Offers quick actions to guide you in your updating. 

Mintlify Doc Writer for Python, JavaScript, TypeScript,
C++, PHP, Java, C#, Ruby & more

AI-powered documentation writer for JavaScript, Python, Java, Typescript & all other languages.

Material Icon Theme

Material Design Icons for Visual Studio Code.

Import Cost

Display import/require package size in the editor.

Image preview

Shows an image preview in the gutter and on hover.

Git Graph

View a Git Graph of your repository, and perform Git actions from the graph.

Color Highlight

Highlight web colors in your editor.
 

change-case

Quickly change the case (camelCase, CONSTANT_CASE, snake_case, etc) of the current selection or current word.

Better Comments

Improve your code commenting by annotating with alerts, informational, TODOs, and more!

Auto Rename Tag

Auto rename paired HTML/XML tags.

Android iOS Emulator

If you are a mobile developer, run the Android emulator and iOS simulator easily from vs code!

Thank you for reading all the way through.

Hope this post was useful to improve your Developer Journey !!

Originally published on: https://blogs.appymango.com/blogs/62703ae8483b05951950af41
item.author: santhoshumapathi

item.title: 与沃尔特一起进行科技冒险 | Tech adventures with Walt
item.description:
昨晚我花了一个多小时试图在一个好朋友的电脑上设置视频聊天。 他的名字是沃尔特，他是一名老年人，住在俄勒冈州。 我居住在加利福尼亚州。 相当艰巨的过程是这样的：

  1. 从 Facetime 开始。 他有一部 iPhone； 我有 MacBook Pro，但没有 iPhone。
  2. 他提到他希望能够在笔记本电脑上进行视频聊天。 当然，听起来很容易。
  3. 尝试在他的手机上通过 Facetime 向他提供指示，从而在他的笔记本电脑上安装 zoom。 这需要一些时间。 他不得不经常将手机摄像头对准屏幕。
  4. 最后安装变焦。 他确实可以访问他的 Gmail 帐户，所以我发送了一个缩放链接。 他设法进去了。
  5. 音频不工作。 一开始我不知道为什么。 他没有静音，他的视频正在播放。 他能听到我的声音。
  6. 我需要访问他的设置。 他共享屏幕，我请求控制。 但是缩放音频很有趣，我无法使用我们的缩放屏幕共享/控件在他的计算机上操作缩放控件。
  7. 叹。 我通过变焦遥控器在他的系统上安装了 TeamViewer。 我得到了一个 TeamViewer 会话，连接起来。
  8. 我们现在有： A. FaceTime 正在运行音频。 变焦定时器用完。 幸运的是，我在他的桌面上创建了一个到缩放室的快捷方式，假设我的缩放链接将通过重新启动缩放保持不变。 我告诉他在我重新启动它时双击它以重新连接到房间。 该链接不起作用。 嗯。 我给他发了一个新链接，他加入了。
  9. 在他的机器上，使用 TeamViewer 进行远程操作，我终于在 Windows 的音频设置深处找到了一些设置，这些设置禁用了他的麦克风。 我打开它。 我们有音频（放大）！
  10. 现在好像这一切还不够，我的互联网一直在跳动和断断续续，视频很烂，可能是因为我们同时流式传输了太多内容。 互联网 borks 和断开连接。 由于某种原因，我无法重新连接到本地 wifi，我们又回到了 FaceTime 电话上，为此大笑。 沃尔特问。 我说谎了。
  11. 我们都准备弃船了。 你只能把泰坦尼克号上的黄铜打磨这么久。 我想到了最后一件事，他在开头提到的事情； 等等……环聊发生了什么？ 那是一回事吗？ 我把它点燃。 我通过电子邮件邀请沃尔特。 他立即连接。

天使此时在天上歌唱。 哈利路亚。 它刚刚工作，第一次。 惊人的。

现在，话虽如此，如果我们用 Meet领导，我们也会遇到同样奇怪的麦克风设置问题。 但是，嘿，我们确实找到了比 Walt 从一开始就立即理解的缩放更易于使用的控件。

这个故事的寓意是……没有道德。

祝你好运与 gamgam 一起缩放。 你会需要它的。 :D :D



原文:

I spent over an hour last night trying to set up video chat on a good friend's computer. His name is Walt and he is a senior citizen and lives in Oregon. I live in California. The quite arduous process kind of went like this:

  1. Start on Facetime. He has an iPhone; I have a MacBook Pro, but not an iPhone.
  2. He mentions he wants to be able to video chat on the laptops. Sure thing, sounds easy enough.
  3. Try to navigate the installation of zoom on his laptop by giving him directions over Facetime on his phone. This takes some time. He has to point the phone camera at the screen frequently.
  4. Finally get zoom installed. He does have access to his Gmail account so I send a zoom link. He manages to get in.
  5. The audio does not work. At first I can't figure out why. He is not muted, his video is on. He can hear me.
  6. I need access to his settings. He shares screen, I request control. But zoom audio is funny and I can't manipulate zoom controls on his computer using our zoom screenshare/control.
  7. Sigh. I install TeamViewer on his system via zoom remote control. I get a TeamViewer session going, get connected.
  8. We now have: A. FaceTime running for audio. B. Zoom running to diagnose the problem. C. TeamViewer running so I have actual granular control of things on his system, including zoom, so I can find out why his outgoing audio is not working.
  9. The zoom timer runs out. Fortunately I have created a shortcut on his desktop to the zoom room, assuming my zoom link will be persistent through restarts of the zoom. I tell him to double click it to reconnect to the room when I restart it. The link does not work. Smh. I email him a new link and he joins.
  10. On his machine, using TeamViewer to remote, I finally find some setting deep in the audio settings in windows that has disabled his mic. I turn it on. We have audio (on zoom)!
  11. Now as if all this wasn't enough, my internet keeps getting jumpy and gappy and the video sucks probably because we are streaming so much stuff simultaneously. The internet borks and disconnects. I cannot reconnect to my local wifi for some reason and we are back to FaceTime on the phones laughing about this. "Is this what we are going to have to go through every time?" asks Walt. "No, just this time." I lie.
  12. We are both ready to abandon ship. You can only polish the brass on the Titanic for so long. I think of one final thing, something he mentioned at the beginning; "My sister talks to her son on Google..."
  13. Wait... whatever happened to Hangouts? Is that a thing? (no) What is it called now? (google meet) Well, may as well give it a shot. I fire it up. I invite Walt via email. He connects immediately.

Angels were singing in heaven at this point. Hallelujah. It just worked, the first time. Amazing.

Now, that being said, if we had led with Meet, we would've had the same weird mic setting problem. But hey, we did find something with easier-to-use controls than zoom that Walt immediately understood from the start.

The moral of the story is... there is no moral.

Good luck zooming with gamgam. You're gonna need it. :D
item.author: aretecore

item.title: 使用 Python 构建 CRM Web 应用程序 | Building a CRM Web App With Python
item.description:
促使我构建这个网络应用程序的动机是我希望能够拥有一个使用我自己想要的问题和数据库结构构建的个人表单应用程序。

我花了一段时间才终于把这几行代码放在一起，但我很高兴我做到了。

这个 CRM Web 应用程序是使用 python 编程、 streamlit和Deta Base构建的，这是一个开源数据库结构，允许您以JSON格式存储数据。

为我们的应用导入必要的库

import streamlit as st import pandas as pd from deta import Deta import json import base64

我们的表格代码

st.header("Pink Data Hub CRM") pink_data = st.sidebar.selectbox("choose:",("Chose","Database", "Database Connection")) deta = Deta(st.secrets["deta_key"]) db = deta.Base("CRM-Records") if pink_data == "Database": with st.form("Submit", clear_on_submit=True): id_name = st.text_input("Company's ID") name = st.text_input("Company's Name") phone = st.text_input("Company's Phone Number") email = st.text_input("Company's Email Address") location = st.text_input("Company's Location") submitted = st.form_submit_button("Submit") if submitted: db.put({"company_id":id_name, "company_name":name, "email_address":email, "location":location}) "---"

查看您收集的数据

if pink_data == 'Database Connection': db_content = db.fetch().items st.write(db_content)

要在 DETA BASE 中检索存储的数据库，请点击此处的链接

节点JS

这是这个项目的回购

您可以将应用程序部署到Streamlit Cloud



原文:

What motivated me build this web app is my desire to be able to have a personal form app built with my own desired questions and database structure.

It took me a while to finally put this few lines of code together, but i am glad i did.

This CRM Web App, is built with python programming, streamlit and Deta Base an open source data base structure that allows you store your data in a JSON Format.

Importing Necessary Libraries For Our App

import streamlit as st
import pandas as pd
from deta import Deta
import json
import base64

Our Form Codes

st.header("Pink Data Hub CRM")

pink_data = st.sidebar.selectbox("choose:",("Chose","Database", "Database Connection"))


deta = Deta(st.secrets["deta_key"])

db = deta.Base("CRM-Records")


if pink_data == "Database":
          with st.form("Submit", clear_on_submit=True):
               id_name = st.text_input("Company's ID")
               name = st.text_input("Company's Name")
               phone = st.text_input("Company's Phone Number")
               email = st.text_input("Company's Email Address")
               location = st.text_input("Company's Location")
               submitted = st.form_submit_button("Submit")
               if submitted:
                     db.put({"company_id":id_name, "company_name":name, "email_address":email, "location":location})

"---"

To View Your Collected Data

if pink_data ==  'Database Connection':
    db_content = db.fetch().items
    st.write(db_content)

To retrieve your stored database in DETA BASE here are links to do so

NODE JS, PYTHON

Here is the repo to this project

You can deploy your app to Streamlit Cloud
